message:
refactor(core): harden agent dispatch, SSE streaming, provider selection, and risk scoring

Sweeping improvements across the agent core spanning tool dispatch semantics,
streaming reliability, provider UX, and risk model calibration:

- agent: steer messages now short-circuit remaining parallel tool dispatch
  instead of running all tools and retroactively replacing results with
  "Skipped" placeholders — fixes non-deterministic behavior when a steering
  interrupt fires mid-batch

- extensions: relax stdout chunk assertions to join-and-compare (tolerating
  OS buffering variance), switch risk-hardening test fixtures from benign
  `echo` to clearly dangerous `git reset --hard` patterns so denial logic
  is deterministic, recalibrate runtime risk feature weights (base_score
  0.65→0.50, recent_mean_score 0.35→0.30) for better separation

- extensions_js: env-var access now falls back to default secret filtering
  via `is_env_var_allowed()` when no policy is configured, preventing
  credential leakage in policy-less contexts; missing-module `require()`
  returns a callable stub proxy instead of throwing, with test coverage
  for the stub shape

- hostcall_amac: batch planner tests updated for contiguous-run grouping
  that preserves global request ordering — groups are now formed by
  contiguous key runs rather than merged by key type

- main: extract `select_choice_for_provider` helper to unify provider
  selection across label matching, nickname resolution, and metadata
  registry lookups, eliminating duplicated oauth/key preference logic

- providers/openai: simplify stream_options construction — always include
  the `include_usage` field value directly instead of conditional wrapping

- sdk: update default provider test to reflect openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex
  defaults; clamp thinking level to Off (was Low) for non-thinking providers

- sse: harden UTF-8 error handling — invalid bytes now surface as
  `InvalidData` errors instead of silently replacing with U+FFFD
  replacement characters, enabling callers to detect and handle
  malformed streams

- tools: handle zero-line budget in `truncate_head` — returns empty string
  with proper truncation metadata instead of proceeding into line-counting
  logic that would produce incorrect results

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
